comment: Rare map events tied to special spell domains. Each opens a domain-exclusive shop on success — the only way to acquire those spells. Realm-agnostic; availability controlled by map_config.

the_pit.title: The Pit
the_pit.text:
The path descends into a vast muddy hollow. Dozens of mudlings — squat, glistening figures of animated muck — mill about in the thick brown sludge below. They have a crude camp of sorts: stacked bones, offerings of rusted metal, a clumsily drawn circle of wet earth in the center.

One large mudling turns its featureless face toward you. A sound like bubbling tar rises from its throat. The others still.

choices.text: Offer a valuable piece of equipment as tribute.

outcome.text: The large mudling reaches out a dripping limb and takes the offering. It sinks it reverently into the central circle. The colony burbles with apparent satisfaction. The elder turns back to you, willing to share what it knows.

choices.text: Demonstrate mastery of earth. Let them see what the deep stone knows.

outcome.text: You call stone and soil to your hands and let the deep vibration speak. The mudlings go utterly still. Then, one by one, they prostrate themselves in the mud. The elder rises and presses both palms against your chest — it recognises a peer, and is willing to teach.

choices.text: Stage an impromptu mud puppet play for their entertainment.

outcome_success.text: Heroes, villains, a tragic love, a pratfall — the mudlings erupt into a sound like a dozen mud geysers firing at once. Applause. The elder mudling, visibly moved, gestures toward the central circle. You are welcome to learn.

outcome_failure.text: Your puppet stumbles. Your plot collapses. The mudlings watch politely for a moment, then begin pelting you with clods. You flee the pit covered head to toe — but oddly, not harmed.

crystal_cave.title: Crystal Cave
crystal_cave.text:
The cave mouth is unremarkable — a crack in a hillside. Inside, it opens into something else entirely. Formations of crystal the height of houses catch what little light enters and multiply it into thousands of beams, filling the space with cold, sourceless radiance.

At the centre, seated in perfect stillness on bare stone, is a man. He is old. He is completely naked. He is meditating, and does not appear to notice you at all.

choices.text: Sit down and meditate alongside him.

outcome.text:
You sit. The cave does the rest. After some time — minutes or hours, it is impossible to say — the yogi opens his eyes. He looks at you the way people look at someone they recognise from a long time ago.

'You already know some of this,' he says. 'Let me show you the rest.'

choices.text: Ask him about the secrets of this cave.

outcome.text:
He opens one eye. He looks you over slowly. Then he smiles — not warmly, exactly, but as if amused by something he's been waiting to see.

'You speak the right language,' he says. 'Then you will understand what I am about to tell you.'

choices.text: Leave him offerings and ask for a blessing.

outcome.text:
You set down what you have brought — a little gold, a little food — and bow. The yogi does not open his eyes. But something in the cave shifts. The light settles differently on you.

You feel cleaner, somehow. Sharper. You carry the blessing forward.

You raise your weapon. He opens his eyes.

The cave is gone. You are standing on the hillside in the open air. There is no crack in the rock, no crystal, no yogi. Only a very faint impression of sadness that may be yours or may be his — it is difficult to tell.

glass_mountain.title: Glass Mountain
glass_mountain.text:
A mountain of glass rises from the plain — not metaphor, not illusion. Pure fused silica, facets the size of houses catching the light and throwing it in every direction. The surface is perfectly smooth and perfectly vertical. There is no path up. There is no grip.

At the summit, something glitters.

choices.text: Attempt to scale the glass face.

outcome_success.text: Your fingers find edges too fine to see, your weight shifts by instinct rather than thought. Slowly, impossibly, you climb. At the top, the mountain teaches you something about what it is made of.

outcome_failure.text: You slip. The glass offers nothing to hold and less to forgive — you slide and tumble, cutting yourself on the way down. The mountain stands unchanged.

choices.text: Let the air carry you up.

outcome.text: You step off the ground and let the wind take you — not flying, exactly, but drifting upward along the face of the mountain, close enough to see your own distorted reflection in every facet. At the summit you understand something about the nature of glass: it is air that forgot how to move.

choices.text: The mountain is not for you today. Turn back.

outcome.text: You walk away. The glass catches your retreating reflection and holds it for a moment, then lets it go.

the_smoking_mirror.title: The Smoking Mirror
the_smoking_mirror.text:
In a clearing where ash drifts like snow, a mirror stands alone — black obsidian framed in tarnished silver, taller than a person, tilted slightly as if to catch a sky it has no interest in showing you.

The surface does not reflect. It smokes. Slow dark coils rise from the glass and dissipate without smell or warmth. Where your reflection should be, there is only depth — and somewhere in that depth, something moves.

outcome.text:
You hold its gaze. The smoke parts. You see yourself — but wrong: every flaw given a face, every hidden thing laid bare. Something dissolves. Something else rises in its place.

When you look away, you understand smoke in a way you did not before. The price was a piece of yourself. The mirror does not seem to care.

choices.text: Recognize the mirror for what it is. Hold up your own mirror to face it.

outcome.text:
You have seen this described in the old texts: a Tezcatlipoca-mirror, a devourer of reflections. You produce your own mirror — polished bronze, consecrated — and hold it to face the black glass.

The two mirrors lock. The smoke rushes between them in a furious loop, finds no victim, and exhausts itself. The obsidian surface is still. In the silence, the knowledge of smoke rises in you like cooling ash.

choices.text: Strike the mirror. Whatever is in there, drag it out.
